sentence: I wish I ______ taller. I can't reach the top shelf.
explanation: Wish + past simple (were) for unreal present situations. We use <were> for all persons in formal English.
tip: You're not tall enough — what form of 'be' expresses this unreal present wish?

sentence: I wish I ______ speak Japanese fluently.
explanation: Wish + <could> for abilities we don't have in the present.
tip: You can't speak Japanese — what past modal expresses this wished-for ability?

sentence: If only I ______ studied harder for the exam.
explanation: If only + past perfect for regrets about the past. <Had studied> = past perfect.
tip: You didn't study hard enough — what auxiliary starts the past perfect for regret?

sentence: I wish you ______ stop making so much noise.
explanation: Wish + <would> for complaints about other people's annoying habits.
tip: Someone is being noisy and you want them to change — what auxiliary expresses this?

sentence: She wishes she ______ more free time to read.
explanation: Wish + past simple for unreal present situations. <Had> = past simple of <have>.
tip: She doesn't have enough free time — past simple after 'wish'.

sentence: If only we ______ bought that house when it was cheap.
explanation: If only + past perfect for past regrets. Stronger emotion than <wish>.
tip: You didn't buy it and now regret it — past perfect for past regret.

sentence: I wish it ______ raining. I want to go outside.
explanation: Wish + <would> + base verb for wanting a current situation to change.
tip: It's raining and you want it to change — what expresses wanting change?

sentence: He wishes he ______ accepted the job offer last month.
explanation: Wish + past perfect for regrets about past decisions.
tip: He didn't accept and now regrets it — past perfect for past regret.

sentence: I wish I ______ live closer to the beach.
explanation: Wish + <could> for impossible or difficult present wishes.
tip: Living near the beach isn't possible right now — what modal expresses this?

sentence: If only the traffic ______ so bad every morning.
explanation: If only + past simple (negative) for complaining about present reality.
tip: The traffic IS bad — negate 'were' to wish it wasn't.

sentence: I wish I ______ eaten so much at dinner. I feel terrible.
explanation: Wish + past perfect (negative) for regretting a past action.
tip: You ate too much and regret it — negate the past perfect.

sentence: She wishes her boss ______ give her more responsibility.
explanation: Wish + <would> for wanting someone else to change their behaviour.
tip: She wants her boss to change — what auxiliary expresses this desire?

sentence: If only I ______ younger, I would learn to play the piano.
explanation: If only + <were> for unreal present wishes about personal characteristics.
tip: You're not young — what form of 'be' expresses this hypothetical wish?

sentence: I wish we ______ gone to the concert. I heard it was amazing.
explanation: Wish + past perfect for missed opportunities in the past.
tip: You didn't go and now regret it — past perfect for past regret.

sentence: He wishes he ______ drive. It would make his commute easier.
explanation: Wish + <could> for abilities the person doesn't have.
tip: He can't drive — what past modal expresses this wished-for ability?

sentence: If only the neighbours ______ play loud music at night.
explanation: If only + <wouldn't> for complaining about other people's annoying habits.
tip: The neighbours play loud music and you want them to stop — negate 'would'.

sentence: I wish I ______ known about the sale. I would have bought a new laptop.
explanation: Wish + past perfect for regret about not knowing something in the past.
tip: You didn't know about the sale — past perfect for past regret.

sentence: She wishes she ______ living in a bigger city.
explanation: Wish + past continuous (<were living>) for unreal present situations in progress.
tip: She's not living in a big city — 'were' completes the past continuous wish.

sentence: If only I ______ brought an umbrella. Now I'm completely wet.
explanation: If only + past perfect for regretting not doing something in the past.
tip: You didn't bring an umbrella — past perfect for past regret.

sentence: I wish the government ______ do more about climate change.
explanation: Wish + <would> for wanting institutions or authorities to take action.
tip: You want the government to change its behaviour — what auxiliary expresses this?

sentence: He wishes he ______ rich so he could help more people.
explanation: Wish + <were> for unreal present states. Used for all persons in formal English.
tip: He's not rich — what form of 'be' expresses this unreal wish?

sentence: If only we ______ started the project earlier.
explanation: If only + past perfect for regretting delayed action.
tip: You started late — past perfect for past regret.

sentence: I wish my phone ______ have such a short battery life.
explanation: Wish + past simple (negative) for complaining about present reality.
tip: Your phone has short battery life — negate the past simple to wish it didn't.

sentence: If only she ______ told me the truth from the beginning.
explanation: If only + past perfect for regretting someone else's past action.
tip: She didn't tell the truth — past perfect for past regret.

sentence: I wish I ______ afford to travel more often.
explanation: Wish + <could> for financial limitations in the present.
tip: You can't afford it — what past modal expresses this wished-for ability?
